passives.name: Between the Ribs
passives.effects: You can now identify the ''Weakpoints'' of nearby enemy champions, marked as an arc around them and pointing towards a specific direction. ''Weakpoints'' on targets take 4 seconds to become exposed and linger for {{fd|11.25}} seconds afterwards. Dealing damage while you are facing an exposed ''Weakpoint'' will consume it to deal {{as|'''bonus''' true damage}} equal to {{as|3%|hp}} {{as|(+ 3% per 100 AD)}} {{as|(+ 2% per 100 AP)}} {{as|of the target's '''maximum''' health}} and grant you {{as|{{pp|50 to 75|key=%}} '''bonus''' movement speed}} for {{fd|1.5}} seconds. Once a target's ''Weakpoint'' is consumed or expires, you identify a new ''Weakpoint'' on them.

effects_structured.raw: You can now identify the ''Weakpoints'' of nearby enemy champions, marked as an arc around them and pointing towards a specific direction
''Weakpoints'' on targets take 4 seconds to become exposed and linger for {{fd 11.25}} seconds afterwards

Dealing damage while you are facing an exposed ''Weakpoint'' will consume it to deal {{as '''bonus''' true damage}} equal to {{as 3% hp}} {{as (+ 3% per 100 AD)}} {{as (+ 2% per 100 AP)}} {{as of the target's '''maximum''' health}} and grant you {{as {{pp 50 to 75 key=%}} '''bonus''' movement speed}} for {{fd 1.5}} seconds

schema_notes.effects_structured: Parsed from passive/active text for simulation use.
context_notes: Execution model: weakpoints are directional arcs on nearby enemies; they expose after a 4-second delay and remain available for 11.25 seconds unless consumed earlier.

Player-visible behavior: damaging an exposed weakpoint while facing its direction consumes that weakpoint immediately, applies the bonus true-damage payload, and grants short movement speed before a new weakpoint is assigned.
Post-rework Arena patch history confirms the current AD/AP scaling model and higher movement-speed floor used in this file.

Availability/economy note: this distributed item is Arena-scoped and acquired through distributed systems (for example anvils, augments, or round rewards) rather than standard shop purchase; Tier-1 datasets expose reconciliation economy values (total 1000 / sell 400).
